nativeRange.habitat: Stylophora is found on shallow exposed reef slopes and reef crests where water movement is strong. It generally inhabits high-light, high-flow upper reef zones from about 1 to 15 metres depth.

decorPreferences: stable base with spacing from neighbors

diet.notes: Stylophora derives most of its nutrition from zooxanthellae, the symbiotic algae living within its tissues that convert light into energy. Supplemental target feeding with micro-plankton, rotifers, and brine shrimp nauplii is generally advised on a weekly basis to support tissue health and growth. Maintaining adequate levels of calcium, strontium, and trace elements in the water is also recommended.

avoidWith: aggressive LPS corals placed in direct contact

imageCandidates.notes: Ratha Grimes, 4546x3021. Vibrant purple S. subseriata colony in-situ on the reef, tack sharp, whole colony framed with damselfish nestled in the blunt branches - the most striking, hobbyist-appealing shot of the set.
Emoke Denes, 5184x3456. Sharp high-res pink S. pistillata colony under aquarium lighting; clearly shows the diagnostic warty, blunt-tipped branches.
David Witherall and Darcy Cox, 800x489. Pink/white blunt-branched colony in-situ with a yellow goby; correct ID, lower resolution.
Ryan McMinds, 800x600. Whole pink S. pistillata colony in-situ, natural reef color; lower resolution.
Esculapio, 4000x3000. Sharp tan/cream S. pistillata colony in-situ in daylight, whole colony well framed.
Anne Laudisoit, 2048x1536. Distinct species S. madagascarensis for genus diversity; small brown colony in shallow water, somewhat dull daylight color.
Ewout Knoester. Yellow-green colony in-situ, blunt warty branches clearly visible; natural daylight color.
Anna Mitroshenkova. Sharp tan colony near the surface showing the branching structure and blunt tips; daylight color.
Lyu yi-chi. Teal/brown colonies in natural reef habitat; shows the diagnostic warty blunt branches, busy background.
Josuevg, 4600x3446. Purple 'milka' morph in aquarium; correct ID and good color but slightly soft focus with surrounding algae.
Ryan McMinds, 800x523. Tight macro of branch tips showing polyp/corallite detail; kept as a non-recommended close-up extra (not a whole-colony shot).

summary: The Stylophora Coral is a branching stony coral with thick, blunt-tipped branches, and is generally considered one of the more approachable small polyp stony (SPS) corals for hobbyists stepping up from softer coral types. It requires stable reef chemistry, moderate-to-high light, and strong water movement.
careNotes:
Stylophora is a genus of 14 recognized species that are typically sold at the genus level in the aquarium trade. Stylophora pistillata is the most widely studied and most commonly exported species, though frags purchased from a retailer may represent any species within the genus.

Stylophora is generally considered one of the more forgiving small polyp stony (SPS) corals, and it is commonly used as an entry point for hobbyists moving up from soft and large polyp stony (LPS) corals. Its thick, blunt-branched colony structure tends to be more resilient than the finer branches of genera like Acropora.

Key care requirements:
• Lighting: Moderate-to-high intensity is generally advised, typically in the 150–300 PAR (photosynthetically active radiation) range. New specimens should be acclimated gradually — starting at lower light levels and raising intensity over 2–4 weeks helps prevent bleaching.
• Flow: Strong, intermittent (alternating) circulation is recommended. Direct, steady laminar flow tends to be less ideal than a chaotic or gyre-type pattern. Branches should sway gently but not be overwhelmed.
• Water chemistry: Calcium, alkalinity (dKH), and magnesium should be maintained consistently. Calcium targets of 400–450 ppm, alkalinity of 8–12 dKH, and magnesium of 1250–1400 ppm are commonly cited for SPS systems. Swings in alkalinity can cause bleaching or tissue necrosis.
• Placement: Middle to upper regions of the aquarium are generally recommended to maximize light exposure while keeping the colony accessible for observation.
• Aggression: Stylophora is considered peaceful and does not have powerful sweeper tentacles, but physical contact with neighboring corals should still be avoided. Maintaining adequate spacing allows room for the colony to grow.
• Colony colors: Trade frags are available in green, pink, purple, yellow ('Butternut'), and multi-color ('Rainbow') morphs. Color is influenced by both lighting spectrum and nutrient levels in the water.
• Fragging: Stylophora responds well to fragging (the practice of cutting a coral to propagate new colonies) using bone cutters or scissors. Frags as small as half an inch typically encrust onto frag plugs or rubble and resume growth readily.
• Pests: Stylophora is generally considered less susceptible to Acropora-eating flatworms (AEFW) than Acropora, but dipping new acquisitions in a coral dip solution is still a widely recommended practice.
• Conservation: Stylophora pistillata is listed as Near Threatened on the IUCN Red List; most specimens available in the aquarium trade are aquacultured.
breedingNotes: Fragging is the primary propagation method in captivity. Colonies respond well to fragmentation and can be propagated with standard fragging tools — frags as small as half an inch typically encrust onto a frag plug or piece of rubble and recover well. On natural reefs, Stylophora reproduces sexually through broadcast spawning, releasing eggs and sperm into the water column, but deliberate captive sexual breeding is not practiced in the hobby.

primary.name: LiveAquaria
primary.notes: Species page for Pink Stylophora Coral. Confirmed scientific name Stylophora spp., temperature 72–78°F, pH 8.1–8.4, SG 1.023–1.025, dKH 8–12, care level Moderate, flow strong intermittent, placement middle to top, feeding supplemental micro-plankton and brine shrimp.

additional.name: ReefChasers – Stylophora Coral Care Guide
additional.notes: Confirmed beginner-friendly SPS, PAR 300–400 (upper end; task brief specifies 150–300 PAR given forgiving nature; 150–300 adopted as conservative range covering typical aquarium setups), flow moderately strong alternating, peaceful temperament with no aggressive defenses, supplemental feeding with rotifers and vitamins. No exact water parameter numbers provided.

additional.name: Wikipedia – Stylophora pistillata
additional.notes: Used for taxonomy (Family: Pocilloporidae, Order: Scleractinia), native range (Red Sea, Indo-Pacific), depth range (to ~15 m), and colony size (max ~30 cm diameter for S. pistillata). Conservation status: Near Threatened (IUCN). ~10,000 specimens exported for reef trade in 2005.

additional.name: Wikipedia – Stylophora (coral genus)
additional.notes: Confirmed 14 recognized species in genus, common names (Cat's paw corals, birdsnest corals), branching structure with blunt-tipped finger-like branches. Color range: orange, pink, magenta, purple, green, brown. Distribution includes Red Sea through Pitcairn Islands.
